es.literal: to throw firewood on the fire
es.meaning: To make a tense situation worse.
es.example: No eches más leña al fuego, ya están bastante enfadados.
es.exampleTranslation: Don't add fuel to the fire, they're angry enough already.

it.literal: to throw gasoline on the fire
it.meaning: To inflame an already tense situation.
it.example: Dire questo ora significa gettare benzina sul fuoco.
it.exampleTranslation: Saying that now means pouring fuel on the fire.

bridge.note: Same image, escalating fuel: Spanish adds wood, Italian adds gasoline. gettare recurs (see 0004) — a verb worth banking.

review.notes: Cue drafted by Claude 2026-07-20 from the entry's meaning and bridge — English situational framing, not a language claim, so it needs editorial taste rather than native review. Override freely.

cue: How do you warn someone they're making it worse?
